about.label: About Us
about.title: Who We Are
about.description: We are a church family committed to worship, discipleship, and serving our community. Our mission is to share the gospel of Jesus Christ and grow together in faith, love, and service.

background.title: Background of Local Church
background.body: Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers' Church is a Pentecostal church in Ethiopia. It originated in the mid-1960s E.T (around 1965 E.T) from a prayer meeting held at Addis Ababa University. The church was officially established in 1967 E.T. Bole Full Gospel Believers' Church is one of the local congregations under the Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers' Church, located in southeastern Addis Ababa and its surrounding area. The congregation began in 1989 E.T as a prayer house (a developing church) and became an established parish in 2000 E.T. Guided by the Holy Bible and empowered by the Holy Spirit, and by receiving leadership direction under the Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers' Church, the congregation continues working to advance the Kingdom of God.

faithSections.title: Vision
paragraphs: To see disciples of Jesus Christ whose lives are transformed and who are filled with the Holy Spirit in Ethiopia and beyond Ethiopia.

faithSections.title: Mission
paragraphs: To strive to produce disciples of Jesus Christ whose lives are transformed and who are filled with the Holy Spirit in Ethiopia and beyond Ethiopia.

faithSections.title: Purpose
items: Preaching the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ. (Mark 16; 15)
Making disciples who are baptized in the Holy Spirit who believe in the gospel. (Matt. 28: 19)
Perform activities to provide comprehensive service (James 1: 26)

items.title: Loving
items.desc: Everything that is done without true love is futile, because God is love that everyone should have love for God, for his friends, and even for those who hate him (I Cor. 13 Matt. 22: 35-40).

items.title: Unity/Synergy
items.desc: The Lord Jesus Christ prayed for the unity of the Church today. All worship and service without the unity of spirit and heart have no effect. Therefore, the Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers Church believes in the unity of believers, evangelical churches and teaches this (John 17: 21, 21; Eph). I Corinthians 12 ;4-31; Phil 2.11-10 Acts 9 : 31; Acts 2 : 4)

items.title: Universal Service
items.desc: the physical, social and spiritual of human beingsServe to meet needs (Luke 2:52; James 1: 27).

items.title: Early Pentecostalism
items.desc: The Pentecostalism as prophesied by the prophet Joel from the early church, as evidenced by the book of Acts. It is a sacred practice that has been in the lives of believers ever since. Therefore, first and foremost to be baptized in the Holy Spirit and she admit that the sign is to speak in tongues (Acts 10, 44-45 (Acts 19: 1).

items.title: Holiness
items.desc: holiness is a sign that we are dedicated to God. It is impossible to see and enjoy. Therefore, Ethiopian Full Gospel Believer Church believes that believers must be dedicated in all thoughts and actions. (Colossians 3: 5-10; I Peter 1: 1515; Hebrews 12: 14); Galatians 5: 22)

items.title: Partnership
items.desc: The kingdom of God is one kingdom. The Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers Church appeared in every form and language of the born-again believer. Respectfully, regardless of geography; from the other sharing and supporting the gospel of Christ with the community and she believes based on (Ephesians 1: 4; Ephesians 4:1; 1 Corinthians 12: 14: 14-31; Romans 15: 26: 26-27).

items.title: Fasting and Prayer
items.desc: She received the power of the Holy Spirit to help them. When the Church began preaching Gospel, God did many wonderful things. She endured persecution and suffering through fasting and prayer. Therefore Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers Church became a special place for fasting and prayer according to (Acts 1; 14; 2; Acts 4; 29) that she values it.

items.title: Ministry of Service
items.desc: The Lord Jesus Christ said,Let him teach, as he said, and teach in his ministry Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers Church. Every servant should have the heart of ministry, following the example of Christ and she believes and deserves it (John 13: 13); Mark 10:44 – 45; Philippians 2: 1)

items.title: Righteousness and Justice
items.desc: Justices are the foundations of the Kingdom of Christ in Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers Church, works to make righteousness and Justice for all mankind based on (Matthew 5: 20; Luke 3)

items.title: Loyalty
items.desc: We believe in biblical devotion, soundness of mind, and morality.

items.title: Accountability
items.desc: The quality of work and communication at all levels of the Church is exemplary and we emphasize accountability by believing that it exists according to (Exodus 18, 19-21).

items.title: Transcendence
items.desc: Ethiopian Full Gospel Believers Church believes that everything is naked and exposed before the eyes of God. The work that the church does in God’s house is clear. She believed in Him (John 3: 20), II Corinthians 4: 2, 2, 13).

items.title: Excellency
items.desc: the ultimate goal of the mission is to make believers Disciples of Christ. Education is also important for making disciples and the theological study to help believers grow into Christ likeness. It is important to provide quality, supportive education. Therefore,we struggle to maintain academic excellence by providing quality of education based on (Matthew 28: 19-20)

statement.title: Statement of Faith
statement.description: Core beliefs and doctrinal foundations of the church.
items: We believe that the Bible, which contains the sixty-six books of the Old and New Testaments, is the inspired Word of God, inerrant, infallible, and the final authority in all matters of faith and practice, living and effective today.
We believe in God the Father, God the Son, and God the Holy Spirit, one God.
We believe in the absolute authority and sovereignty of God in creation, preservation, revelation, redemption, and the final judgment.

We believe that our Lord Jesus Christ was sent from the Father, conceived by the Holy Spirit, born of the Virgin Mary, fully man and fully God, died on the cross as a ransom for mankind, was buried, rose bodily from the dead on the third day, ascended to the Father, and is now at the right hand of the Father, interceding for us, and will return in glory and majesty to judge the living and the dead.
We believe that the Holy Spirit is fully God, possessing all the attributes of God, and is worthy of worship and adoration.
We believe that man was created in the image of God, fell into sin, and is therefore guilty and subject to God's wrath and judgment.

We believe that man can be saved from the debt, power, and punishment of sin only through the redemptive work of the sinless Son of God, Jesus Christ, as man's substitute. Therefore, we believe that a sinner is justified by God's grace only through faith in Jesus Christ.
We believe that the Holy Spirit works in the life of a sinner to repent of his sins, believe in Jesus Christ, and become a new creation.

We believe that the Holy Spirit dwells in the life of the believer, that the believer receives power when he is baptized in the Holy Spirit, and that he speaks in tongues and is filled with the Holy Spirit on a regular basis.
We believe that the Holy Spirit continues to work today to build up the church by imparting various gifts of grace to believers, healing the sick, casting out demons, and performing miracles.

We believe that the Church, by fulfilling the Great Commission entrusted to her by Christ, can do all that is necessary for the building up of the body of Christ by preaching the Gospel to all people and making disciples of believers. We believe that the Holy Spirit gives apostles, prophets, evangelists, shepherds, and teachers, as well as other ministry gifts.
We believe in one holy Church, the Body of Christ, in which all who are born again of the Spirit of God are found.

We believe that, as commanded by our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, the born-again believer is to be baptized in water in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, in symbol of his death and resurrection, and to partake of the Lord's Supper, to symbolize his death and resurrection until the Lord's return, and to symbolize his fellowship with the Lord and the believers.
We believe in the resurrection of the dead, the eternal judgment of sinners, and the eternal life of believers.
